Home»‘Hypocrites’: SA takes ‘credit’ for Botswana’s Tebogo, after shunning Chidimma

X users are calling out South Africans praising Botswana’s gold-medallist Tebogo, hours after celebrating Chidimma’s Miss SA exit.

South Africans on social media are calling out the “hypocrisy” of others who are celebrating Botswana Olympic sprinter Letsile Tebogo, who won gold in the 200m race on Thursday, 8 August. This, after seemingly celebrating finalist Chidimma Adetshina’s early exit from the Miss SA pageant

The 23-year-old womanannounced her decision to withdraw from the competition,citing concerns for her safety and well-being.

Over the past few weeks, Chidimma has made headlines over her African parentage, which includes a Nigerian father and an alleged Mozambican mother.

The Miss SA finale takes place on Saturday, 10 August, at the SunBet Arena in Pretoria.

On Thursday, Chidimma Adetshina announced her decision to step down as a Top 10 finalist at the Miss SA pageant.

This followed weeks ofsuffering xenophobic and Afrophobic comments on social mediaand even being labelled a “Nigerian.”

Chidimma’s announcement came shortly after Minister of Home Affairs Leon Schreiber’s statement of a preliminary investigation that claims her mother may have committed fraud and identity theft when registering her birth.

Just hours later, Letsile Tebogo of Botswana would win gold in the men’s 200m race at the Olympics.

The reception of the two Africans by social media users has been polar opposite in comparison.

On the X platform, many South Africans celebrated fellow “African” Tebogo, some of which earlier condemned Chidimma as a non-South African. Some even attempted to take credit for having a “Tswana” heritage.

Others called out the hypocrisy of the situation.

@CMogoeng:“Tebogo Letsile did it for Botswana, not South Africa. We don’t get to be xenophobic and Afrophobic today and then tomorrow claim other people’s glory because we are in a good mood and it suits us“

@MR_K_R_B:“Claiming Tebogo after winning Gold on the same day after disowning Chidimma doesn’t look good on us.

@Melo22Taro:“Making Chidimma resign from Miss SA when she grew up here as a child (unbeknownst of her mother’s actions) and glorifying a Botswana Olympian gold medalists as if he is South African (clearly not) all within this week is some funny sh*t.”

@angrybird_c:“South Africans are hypocrites. They’re quick to show support for Botswana’s Tebogo because of his win but couldn’t let Chidimma complete her Miss SA run. It’s disgusting.”

Meanwhile, Letsile Tebogo has become the first African and athlete from Botswana, to win gold in the Olympics 200m.

Tebogo beat the likes of Noah Lyle, a favourite who later claimed to have contracted Covid-19.

The humble 21-year-old claimed that while he appreciated the praise, he was unfazed by the instant fame.

He said in apress conferenceafter the race: “It is not a dream come true to me. A medal is just a medal – but what I went through. I would say the medal is a bonus to everybody, but personally, it is not a dream come true.

Tebogo dedicated the victory to his mother, who died in May.

He continued: “’It is not a big deal to me. It means a lot to the team behind me. When we heard about my mom’s passing, I was grateful for them – because personally, I thought my career was over, but they made sure I took each and every day as it came and made sure that I came here with a healthier mind”.

title: SA takes 'credit' for Botswana's Tebogo, celebrates Chidimma's exit
author: Megan van den Heever
description: X users are calling out South Africans praising Botswana's gold-medallist Tebogo, hours after celebrating Chidimma's Miss SA exit.
